description: To continue steadfastly despite obstacles; to maintain effort when challenged. 'Meloa' names the steady going—not dramatic heroism but quiet continuation.
sound_symbolism: The word endures gently. 'Me' centers with steady mind, resolve held. 'Lo' opens with continued effort, path maintained. Then 'a' opens to acceptance of the long way.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mia meloa.' (I persevere.) Related to 'puro' (strength) as action to capacity, 'shimalo' (discipline) as practice to training.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Perseverance as virya—the energy of sustained practice, neither forcing nor giving up.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Patient endurance—the quality that sees seasons through, waits for harvest.
